title: Datadog の 500 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: Most factual claims are supported by recent, official, or high-quality secondary sources.
Claims regarding expected HTTP status codes for authentication and rate limits are generally accurate, though specific mention of 500 for these scenarios could be more nuanced.
Product comparison claims are well-supported by recent comparative articles.
Some community observation-based claims are inherently difficult to ground with official sources.
citation coverage is below the threshold
freshness score is below the threshold
one or more claims are unsupported or unverifiable
no URLs resolved to known official domains
required_actions: Clarify that 401/403 are the primary HTTP status codes for authentication failures, and 429 for rate limiting, while 500 is a less common, but possible, secondary outcome in specific server-side processing failures.
Provide specific examples or references for community reports regarding region-specific routing issues if possible, or rephrase to indicate anecdotal evidence.
Add links to official documentation or primary sources for key claims.
Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.

sources.title: Monitor third-party service statuses from Statuspage - Datadog
sources.claim: The Datadog Statuspage integration exists to track third-party service availability and incidents.

sources.title: Datadog US1 Status
sources.claim: The official status page for Datadog US1 region is https://status.datadoghq.com.

sources.title: API - Datadog US1 Status
sources.claim: Datadog provides an API endpoint `/api/v2/incidents.json` to programmatically access incident information from its status page.

sources.title: Validate API key - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog provides a `/api/v1/validate` endpoint to verify authentication credentials.

sources.title: Validate API key - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: The response from the `/api/v1/validate` endpoint includes a `valid` field (boolean) indicating whether the authentication information is correctly set.

sources.title: Agent Commands - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: The `datadog-agent version` command is used to check the Datadog Agent's version on Linux/macOS.

sources.title: Linux - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: The command `sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install datadog-agent` is used to update the Datadog Agent on Debian/Ubuntu-based Linux systems.

sources.title: Agent - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog recommends updating the Datadog Agent regularly for the latest functionality and fixes, and outdated agents can cause issues.

sources.title: API Endpoints - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog operates in multiple regions (e.g., US, EU, GovCloud) with different API endpoints.

sources.title: Getting Started with Datadog Sites
sources.claim: The API endpoint for the EU region is `https://api.datadoghq.eu`.

sources.title: Datadog Logs API Best Practices - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog API has rate limits, and typically returns a 429 'Too Many Requests' error when limits are exceeded.

sources.title: Submit metrics - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog's API uses HTTP REST principles, with status codes indicating request success or failure. A 500 error generally indicates an internal server error.

sources.title: Send logs - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog's API uses HTTP REST principles, with status codes indicating request success or failure. A 500 error generally indicates an internal server error.

sources.title: API Keys - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog generally returns 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden for invalid or insufficient API/application keys.

sources.title: Limits - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog has organization-level quotas and limits for various features, and exceeding these can lead to issues.

sources.title: Remote Configuration - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: An outdated Datadog Agent querying for remote configuration can encounter HTTP 500 errors.

sources.title: Agent Rate Limits - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog Agent has rate limits related to connections and memory, and exceeding these can lead to issues.

sources.title: New Relic vs DataDog - Features, Pricing, and Performance Compared (2026) | SigNoz
sources.claim: New Relic offers APM, infrastructure monitoring, and log management in a unified platform, with features comparable to Datadog.

sources.title: Datadog vs Dynatrace: Which is Better in 2026? [Hands-On Testing] | SigNoz
sources.claim: Dynatrace is strong in automatic detection and AI-powered anomaly detection, suitable for large-scale microservice environments, and offers rich API call tracing and distributed tracing features.

unsupported_claims: API キーまたはアプリケーションキーが無効、期限切れ、または不完全な場合、Datadog サーバーが認証処理で 500 エラーを返すことがあります。(If an API key or application key is invalid, expired, or incomplete, Datadog servers may return a 500 error during authentication processing.) - While invalid keys cause errors, the primary response is 401/403, not typically 500, unless it triggers a deeper internal server issue during processing.

誤ったリージョンのエンドポイントに送信すると 500 エラーが返される可能性があります。(Sending to an incorrect regional endpoint may result in a 500 error.) - While an error would occur, a 500 is not the most common or expected HTTP status code for this scenario; a 400 Bad Request or a specific routing error is more likely.

Datadog の 500 エラーは公式ドキュメントでは「一時的な障害」として扱われることが多いですが、実際のフォーラムやコミュニティでの報告では、ペイロードサイズの超過やレート制限の巧妙な超過が原因であるケースが大多数です。(Datadog 500 errors are often treated as 'temporary outages' in official documentation, but reports in forums and communities indicate that exceeding payload size or subtle rate limit breaches are the majority of causes.) - This is a meta-claim about documentation vs. community experience and the 'majority' of causes, which is difficult to definitively ground from official sources.

公式の解決策「状態ページを確認して再試行する」だけでは解決しないことが珍しくなく、特に日本国内の利用者からは「EU リージョンを指定しているのに US エンドポイントにリクエストが到達している」という報告も寄せられています。(It is not uncommon for the official solution 'check the status page and retry' to be insufficient, and reports from Japanese users, in particular, mention 'requests intended for the EU region reaching US endpoints.') - This is a claim about community reports and region-specific anecdotal issues, which is difficult to verify with official, broad-scope sources.
